For manual/backup-grade extraction, Cursor community guides and scripts also export from state.vscdb/workspaceStorage to Markdown [3][4].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eGitHub - iksnae/cursor-session at 8a197f6cca7289ccf623dbf739e7aff9ed0c512c · GitHub\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/iksnae/cursor-session/tree/8a197f6cca7289ccf623dbf739e7aff9ed0c512c\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eA command-line tool to extract and export chat sessions from Cursor IDE. No need to click each chat individually.\n[...]\nWorks seamlessly on macOS, Windows, and Linux by directly accessing Cursor’s chat database.\n[...]\nGitHub: GitHub - ibrahim317/cursor-chat-transfer: Transfer your AI chat conversations between Cursor IDE workspaces and devices with an intuitive UI.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003esomogyijanos/cursor-chat-export\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/somogyijanos/cursor-chat-export\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eExport your chats from Cursor to Markdown.\n[...]\n# Cursor Chat Export\n[...]\nThis project provides a command-line interface (CLI) tool to discover and export AI chat data from Cursor. The tool is implemented in &#96;chat.py&#96; and leverages utility classes for querying the database, formatting the chat data, and saving it to files.\n[...]\nCursor's chat history is stored in &#96;sqlite3&#96; databases using &#96;state.vscdb&#96; files. One such file is for one workspace. All the chats for a workspace are saved here in so-called *tabs*, one tab means a single chat.\n[...]\n- **Discover Chats**: Discover all chats from all workspaces and print a few lines of dialogue so one can identify which is the workspace (or chat) one is searching for. It's also possible to filter by text.\n- **Export Chats**: Export chats for the most recent (or a specific) workspace to Markdown files or print them to the command line.\n[...]\nBoth the &#96;discover&#96; and &#96;export&#96; commands will work with this path by default, but you can also provide a custom path any time.\n[...]\n### Export Chats\n[...]\nSee &#96;./chat.py export --help&#96; for general help. Examples:\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sh\n# Help on usage\n./chat.py export --help\n[...]\n# Print all chats of the most recent workspace to the command line\n./chat.py export\n\n# Export all chats of the most recent workspace as Markdown\n./chat.py export --output-dir \"/path/to/output\"\n[...]\n# Export only the latest chat of the most recent workspace\n./chat.py export --latest-tab --output-dir \"/path/to/output\"\n[...]\n# Export only chat No. 2 and 3 of the most recent workspace\n./chat.py export --tab-ids 2,3 --output-dir \"/path/to/output\"\n[...]\n# Export all chats of a specifc workspace\n./chat.py export --output-dir \"/path/to/output\" \"/path/to/workspaces/workspace-dir/state.vscdb\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eThe Ultimate Guide to Exporting and Renaming Cursor IDE Chats (With Step-by-Step Solutions) - Dre Dyson\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://dredyson.com/the-ultimate-guide-to-exporting-and-renaming-cursor-ide-chats-with-step-by-step-solutions/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eAugust 11, 2025\n[...]\nI hit a wall with this exact problem last month. _**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

Sí, se puede. Cursor no trae un botón oficial tipo “Exportar a carpeta del repo”, pero hay varias formas prácticas de guardar prompts y conversaciones en `juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/`.

## Lo que ya tenés armado

En `juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/` ya documentás los temas en `indice_ia.md` (secciones **Claude** y **Cursor**), pero todavía no existen subcarpetas `prompts/` ni `conversaciones/`. Lo natural sería crearlas y numerar los archivos alineados con ese índice, por ejemplo:

```
juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/
├── indice_ia.md
├── prompts/
│   ├── cursor-05-instalar-skills.md
│   └── ...
└── conversaciones/
    ├── cursor-05-instalar-skills.md
    └── ...
```

## Opciones para exportar desde Cursor

### 1. Manual (la más simple y confiable)

Al terminar un chat:

- **Prompts**: copiá solo lo que escribiste vos y guardalo en `prompts/`.
- **Conversaciones**: copiá el intercambio completo (pregunta + respuestas) en `conversaciones/`.

Ventaja: control total del formato para la entrega. Desventaja: es manual.

### 2. Extensión de VS Code / Cursor

Hay extensiones que exportan chats a Markdown:

- **Cursor Chat Exporter** — `Ctrl+Shift+E`, elige conversación y formato `.md`.
- **SpecStory** — exporta historial de Chat y Composer.
- **Cursor Chat Transfer** — más orientado a mover chats entre workspaces.

Podés configurar la ruta por defecto hacia `juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/conversaciones/`.

### 3. Herramientas CLI (export masivo)

Para muchas conversaciones de una vez:

- **[cursor-session](https://github.com/iksnae/cursor-session)** — `cursor-session export --format md`
- **[cursor-chat-export](https://github.com/somogyijanos/cursor-chat-export)** — lee la base SQLite de Cursor y genera `.md`

En Windows, los chats viven en algo como:

`%APPDATA%\Cursor\User\workspaceStorage\`

(cada workspace tiene un `state.vscdb` con el historial).

### 4. Transcripts técnicos del agente (limitado)

Cursor guarda transcripts de agentes en:

`.cursor/projects/.../agent-transcripts/*.jsonl`

Son JSONL con mensajes, tool calls y a veces contenido `[REDACTED]`. Sirven como backup técnico, pero **no son ideales para entregar** tal cual: hay que convertirlos a Markdown legible.

## Cómo separar prompt vs conversación

| Carpeta | Contenido |
|---------|-----------|
| `prompts/` | Solo tus instrucciones (el `<user_query>` o lo que pegaste en el chat) |
| `conversaciones/` | Intercambio completo: tu mensaje + respuestas del asistente |

Ejemplo según tu índice:

- **Cursor 04** → prompt: “Instala estas 5 skills…” → conversación: prompt + respuesta + creación de `skills_instaladas.md`.

## Para Claude (claude.ai)

Es aparte de Cursor:

- Copiar/pegar desde la UI.
- Usar “Share” y guardar el enlace o el contenido exportado.
- Guardar en la misma estructura bajo `prompts/` y `conversaciones/` con prefijo `claude-01-...`, etc.

## Recomendación práctica para la entrega

1. Crear `prompts/` y `conversaciones/` dentro de `ia/entrega-1/`.
2. Al cerrar cada tema, exportar con extensión o copiar manualmente.
3. Nombrar archivos con el número del índice (`cursor-05-...`, `claude-07-...`).
4. Actualizar `indice_ia.md` con el link o nombre del archivo.
5. Opcional: export semanal con CLI como respaldo.

Si querés, en **modo Agent** puedo ayudarte a crear la estructura de carpetas, un template de Markdown y un script que convierta los `.jsonl` de agent-transcripts a archivos legibles en `conversaciones/`. En Ask mode solo puedo orientarte; para implementarlo habría que cambiar a Agent mode.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user cannot locate the Cursor Chat Exporter extension because Cursor's extension marketplace differs from VS Code's, causing some extensions to not appear in searches.</details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

Es normal que no la encuentres: **Cursor Chat Exporter existe en el marketplace de VS Code**, pero **no siempre aparece en el buscador de extensiones de Cursor**. Tiene muy pocas instalaciones (~100) y el marketplace de Cursor no es idéntico al de VS Code.

## Por qué no la ves

1. **Cursor filtra extensiones** — no todas las de VS Code están indexadas.
2. **Nombre distinto** — probá buscar `chat exporter`, `RohanGore`, o el ID exacto: `RohanGore1999.cursor-chat-exporter`.
3. **Puede no estar publicada para Cursor** — solo para VS Code clásico.

## Cómo instalarla igual (desde VSIX)

### Opción A: descargar desde VS Code

1. Abrí **VS Code** (no Cursor).
2. Extensions → buscá **Cursor Chat Exporter**.
3. En la página de la extensión, clic en el ⚙️ → **Download VSIX**.
4. En **Cursor**: `Ctrl+Shift+P` → **Extensions: Install from VSIX...**
5. Elegí el `.vsix` descargado y reiniciá Cursor.

### Opción B: descarga directa

En el marketplace de VS Code, el enlace de descarga suele ser:

`https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/_apis/public/gallery/publishers/RohanGore1999/vsextensions/cursor-chat-exporter/latest/vspackage`

Guardá el archivo (a veces viene como `.zip`; renombralo a `.vsix` si hace falta) e instalalo con **Install from VSIX** en Cursor.

---

## Alternativas más fáciles de encontrar

| Extensión | Qué hace | Cómo buscarla |
|-----------|----------|---------------|
| **SpecStory** | Exporta Chat y Composer a Markdown | `SpecStory` |
| **Cursor Chat Transfer** | Export/import de chats | `cursor chat transfer` |
| **cursor-chat-share** (GitHub) | Export a `.md` | Instalar desde VSIX del repo |

**SpecStory** suele ser la que más gente usa para documentar conversaciones.

---

## Si ninguna extensión funciona: métodos sin extensión

### 1. Copiar manualmente (más confiable para la entrega)

- Seleccioná todo el chat → copiar → pegar en un `.md` en `conversaciones/`.
- El prompt solo → `prompts/`.

### 2. CLI `cursor-session`

```bash
npm install -g cursor-session
cursor-session list
cursor-session export --format md
```

Lee el historial local de Cursor y genera Markdown.

### 3. Archivos JSONL del agente (backup técnico)

Cursor guarda transcripts en:

`C:\Users\nicop\.cursor\projects\c-Users-nicop-OneDrive-Escritorio-Facultad-4toA-o-Electivas-4to-Desarrollo-de-Aplicaciones-Moviles-1C-Juntadas-App\agent-transcripts\`

Son JSONL con tool calls; no son ideales para entregar tal cual, pero sirven como respaldo.

---

## Recomendación práctica

Para tu carpeta `ia/entrega-1/`:

1. Probá **SpecStory** primero (suele aparecer en Cursor).
2. Si no, **copiá manualmente** al cerrar cada tema — es lo que más docentes aceptan.
3. Dejá **Cursor Chat Exporter** como plan C vía VSIX.

Si querés, en **modo Agent** puedo armarte la estructura `prompts/` y `conversaciones/` con templates y un script que convierta los `.jsonl` a Markdown legible.

---

_**User**_

ya tengo spec history como la configuro para que exporte a ia/entrega-1/conversaciones/
Las carpetas prompts/ y conversaciones/ ya existen en el repo — las creamos antes

---

/ &#96;sync&#96; **Cursor IDE** provider fails because it looks for:\n[...]\n&#96;C:\\Users\\ \\AppData\\Roaming\\Cursor\\User\\workspaceStorage&#96;\n[...]\nThat path **does not exist** on my machine. The **actual** Cursor &#96;workspaceStorage&#96; is:\n[...]\n&#96;D:\\apps\\cursor\\current\\data\\user-data\\User\\workspaceStorage&#96;\n[...]\n1. Install/run **portable Cursor** (or Cursor started with &#96;--user-data-dir&#96; pointing outside &#96;%APPDATA%&#96;).\n2. Confirm &#96;workspaceStorage&#96; exists only under the custom dir (not under &#96;%APPDATA%\\Cursor\\...&#96;).\n3. Open a workspace in Cursor with SpecStory enabled; let the extension start CLI &#96;watch&#96; / &#96;sync&#96;.\n4. Observe SpecStory output channel / CLI stderr.\n[...]\nSpecStory should resolve Cursor’s **effective** user data directory (same as the running Cursor instance), e.g.:\n[...]\n- Respect Cursor’s actual &#96;user-data-dir&#96;, **or**\n- Allow a setting / env var such as &#96;CURSOR_USER_DATA_DIR&#96; or &#96;specstory.cursorUserDataPath&#96; pointing to the parent of &#96;User&#96; (or to &#96;User&#96; / &#96;workspaceStorage&#96; explicitly).\n[...]\nCLI appears to assume the **default Windows layout** &#96;%APPDATA%\\Roaming\\Cursor\\User\\workspaceStorage&#96; only, so the **Cursor IDE** watcher never starts for portable installs.\n[...]\nIDE\" error=\"failed to create watcher\n[...]\nto get workspace\n[...]\nworkspace storage directory not found\n[...]\nC:\\Users\n[...]\nAppData\\Roaming\\\n[...]\n\\User\\workspaceStorage\n[...]\nCreate a **directory junction** from &#96;%APPDATA%\\Cursor\\User\\workspaceStorage&#96; to the real &#96;workspaceStorage&#96; path. This is fragile if multiple Cursor installs or layouts are used.\n[...]\n\u003e We just released v1.1.0 of the extension that will take care of the custom user data dir. We are still working on supporting VS Code Insiders and VS Codium.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eInstall our extensions - SpecStory\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://specstory.mintlify.app/specstory/introduction\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eThe SpecStory Extension (changelog) supports both Cursor and GitHub Copilot. {
  "markdown": "Cursor\n\nIntegrations›Cursor\n\nSpecStory integrates seamlessly with [Cursor](https://cursor.com/). Cursor already helps developers move faster with inline edits, multi-line completions, and codebase chat. SpecStory adds the missing layer by capturing why the changes were made.\n\nWith SpecStory enabled, every AI conversation in Cursor is auto-saved as Markdown in your project. Your reasoning, tradeoffs, and design decisions live alongside your code: versioned, reusable, and shareable.\n\n## Install the SpecStory Extension\n\nVerify installation:\n\n- Open the Command Palette (&#96;Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P&#96;).\n- Type&#96;SpecStory&#96;, the commands should appear.\n\nWarning\n\nIf you install SpecStory directly from the [Visual Studio Marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SpecStory.specstory-vscode) website, you may end up with VS Code having the extension but not Cursor. Always install directly from inside Cursor to avoid this issue.\n\n## Verify It Works\n\n- Start a chat with Cursor’s AI.\n- Open your project folder.\n- You should now see a new&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; directory.\n- Inside, you should see a Markdown file of your session, named with a timestamp.\n\nTip\n\nSpecStory is local-first by default. Nothing leaves your machine unless you explicitly share it.\n\n## How SpecStory Enhances Cursor\n\n- Auto-save: Every AI conversations in Cursor are automatically stored in&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; as clean Markdown.\n- Manual saves: From the Command Palette, run&#96;SpecStory: Save AI Chat History&#96; to cherry-pick and combine conversations.\n- Share conversations with a URL: Use&#96;SpecStory: Share AI Chat History&#96; to generate a private, anonymous link at&#96;share.specstory.com&#96;.\n\n- Derived rules for AI: SpecStory can extract project-level rules and custom instructions from your chats, feeding them back into Cursor to improve suggestions.\n\nInfo\n\nRule derivation requires:\n\n- Auto-save enabled.\n- Signed-in SpecStory extension.\n\nInfo\n\nTool coverage includes Cursor tool v2 handlers (edit/read/terminal), plus Read/Write, Apply Patch, Grep, Glob, Ripgrep, Read Lints, and MultiEdit in Markdown exports.\n\n## Best Practices for Cursor Users\n\n- Commit your history: Include&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; in your repo so teammates can review reasoning in PRs.\n- Use manual saves before big changes: Preserve the conversation context before accepting multi-line or agent-driven edits.\n- Leverage derived rules: Cursor’s [Project Rules](https://docs.cursor.com/context/rules-for-ai#project-rules-recommended) evolve automatically when SpecStory is active.\n\n## Why This Matters\n\nCursor accelerates what changes in your codebase. SpecStory ensures you never lose why those changes happened. Together, they give you:\n\n- Faster debugging and onboarding\n- Traceable architectural decisions\n- Cleaner collaboration on AI-assisted code\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n- Extension mismatch: If VS Code shows SpecStory installed but Cursor doesn’t, uninstall and reinstall from Cursor’s built-in Marketplace.\n- No&#96;.specstory&#96; folder: Make sure you have an active project folder open. SpecStory only saves inside projects.\n- Still stuck? File an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/issues) or contact [support@specstory.com](mailto:support@specstory.com).",
  "url": "https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/cursor"
}
```

{
  "markdown": "SpecStory - Visual Studio Marketplace Skip to content\n\nWe use optional cookies to improve your experience on our websites, such as through social media connections, and to display personalized advertising based on your online activity. If you reject optional cookies, only cookies necessary to provide you the services will be used. You may change your selection by clicking “Manage Cookies” at the bottom of the page. [Privacy Statement](https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkId=521839) [Third-Party Cookies](https://aka.ms/3rdpartycookies)\n\nAcceptRejectManage cookies\n\n# SpecStory\n\n## SpecStory\n\n|\n\n21,559 installs\n\n| (9) | Free\n\nSave, export, search and share AI chat history from GitHub Copilot and Cursor\n\nOverviewVersion HistoryQ \u0026 ARating \u0026 Review\n\n# What is SpecStory?\n\n[SpecStory](https://specstory.com/) is a suite of local-first extensions that automatically captures your AI coding sessions as reusable git-friendly markdown. While Git tracks what changed in your code, SpecStory helps capture why it changed, preserving the reasoning and decision-making that drove the intent behind every line. Never lose AI chat history.\n\n### ❗️Important Note\n\nBy default, installing from the Visual Studio Marketplace directly will install SpecStory into VS Code. To instead install SpecStory into Cursor, you must install directly from inside the extensions panel in Cursor (see details on our [documentation site](https://docs.specstory.com/specstory/introduction#cursor)).\n\n## ✨ What the SpecStory extension does\n\n### 📝 Automatic local capture\n\nSpecStory automatically saves your AI conversations to&#96;.specstory/history/&#96;(configurable in settings) in your project directory. So your prompts stay with your code.\n\nToggle auto-save on/off in settings to match your workflow.\n\n### 🤖 Generate AI rules from your conversations\n\nTransform your chat history into [Cursor Rules](https://docs.cursor.com/context/rules) or [Copilot Instructions](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/customizing-copilot/adding-repository-custom-instructions-for-github-copilot). SpecStory uses your interaction data to create and maintain context files that keep your AI on track.\n\nCustomize the structure and SpecStory adapts to your preferences.\n\n### 💾 Selective saving\n\nUse Command Palette (CMD/Ctrl-Shift-P) →&#96;SpecStory: Save AI Chat History&#96; to manually save specific conversations when auto-save is off.\n\n### 🔗 Share conversations\n\nShare your problem-solving process with teammates. Command Palette →&#96;SpecStory: Share AI Chat History&#96; creates anonymous shareable links to your conversations.\n\n## 🎮 Control panel\n\nAccess the SpecStory panel to manage histories, configure settings, and monitor auto-save status. Find it in the sidebar or via&#96;View: Open View \u003e SpecStory&#96; in the command palette.\n\n## 🛠️ For Developers\n\nEnable&#96;specstory.showDeveloperTools&#96; in VS Code settings to access:\n\n1. Debug logging in the SpecStory output channel\n2. Cursor sqlite DB explorer in your Primary Sidebar with:\n\n- Search: Command Palette →&#96;SpecStory (Developer Tools): Search VS Code State&#96; or click the magnifying glass icon\n- Refresh: Command Palette →&#96;SpecStory (Developer Tools): Refresh VS Code State&#96; or use the refresh button\n- Copy values: Hover over any value and click the copy icon\n\n## ☁️ SpecStory Cloud\n\nSpecStory Cloud is a new progressive enhancement of our local first extension suite that unifies all your AI conversations from Cursor, VS Code, Claude Code, and BearClaude into one searchable place.\n\n### 🚀 Quick Start\n\n#### For VS Code/Cursor users:\n\n1. Sign up at [cloud.specstory.com](https://cloud.specstory.com/)\n2. Open Command Palette →&#96;SpecStory: Open Cloud Sync Configuration&#96;\n\nYou'll see this screen and then follow the authentication prompts to sync your first project:\n\n#### For Claude Code users:\n\n1. Sign up at [cloud.specstory.com](https://cloud.specstory.com/)\n2. Authenticate:&#96;specstory auth login&#96;\n3. Sync your conversations:&#96;specstory sync&#96;\n\n### ✨ Why Cloud?\n\n- Search everywhere: Full-text search across all your AI chats\n- Privacy first: Nothing syncs without your explicit permission\n- All tools in one place: Unify conversations from Cursor, VS Code, Claude Code, and more\n\n## ✨ We're always shipping something new\n\n- Interested in a minimalist approach for turning markdown specs into software?\n- Try our latest product for macOS at [bearclaude.com](https://bearclaude.com/)\n\n## 📚 Documentation \u0026 Community\n\n- Find our docs chock full of use cases and a getting started guide to master your craft @ [docs.specstory.com](https://docs.specstory.com/).\n- Join our [slack community](https://join.slack.com/t/specstory/shared_invite/zt-2vq0274ck-MYS39rgOpDSmgfE1IeK9gg) to connect with other software composers!\n\n## 🔮 Issue Reporting and Feature Requests\n\n- Found a bug? Have a feature request? Please log it on our [issues page on GitHub](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/issues).\n- For any other issue please email us at [support@specstory.com](mailto:support@specstory.com).\n\n## 🔒 Privacy\n\nSpecStory collects usage data to help improve the product. This can be disabled in the extension by unchecking&#96;SpecStory: Help Us Improve&#96; in your SpecStory extension settings.\n\nThis setting does not affect web analytics. For more information about what data we collect and how we use it, please see our [privacy policy](https://specstory.com/privacy) and [data privacy docs](https://docs.specstory.com/privacy) which provide detail by feature.",

[[EXTENSION/CLI] SpecStory VSCode extension failed to save new conversations after an update of GitHub Copilot with an error &#96;$.trim is not a function&#96; · Issue #138 · specstoryai/getspecstory](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/issues/138)\n\nSynthesis:\nSpecStory’s CLI/conversion output (history markdown) defaults to./.specstory/history inside the current project, and can be customized via the local_sync.output_dir setting (the CLI equivalent is --output-dir). For example, set output_dir in config to ~/.specstory/history, or run the CLI with --output-dir \"~/.specstory/history\" [1].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003especstory-cli/README.md at dev · specstoryai/getspecstory\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/blob/dev/specstory-cli/README.md\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Custom output directory for markdown files.\n# Default: ./.specstory/history (relative to the project directory)\n# output_dir = \"~/.specstory/history\" # equivalent to --output-dir \"~/.specstory/history\"\n[...]\n### Configuration Options\n[...]\n| Section | Option | Default | Description |\n|-------------------|-------------------|----------------------|--------------------------------------------|\n| &#96;[local_sync]&#96; | &#96;enabled&#96; | &#96;true&#96; | Write local markdown files |\n| &#96;[local_sync]&#96; | &#96;output_dir&#96; | &#96;.specstory/history&#96; | Custom output directory for markdown files |\n| &#96;[local_sync]&#96; | &#96;local_time_zone&#96; | &#96;false&#96; | Use local timezone for timestamps |\n| &#96;[cloud_sync]&#96; | &#96;enabled&#96; | &#96;true&#96; | Sync sessions to SpecStory Cloud |\n| &#96;[logging]&#96; | &#96;debug_dir&#96; | &#96;.specstory/debug&#96; | Custom output directory for debug data |\n| &#96;[logging]&#96; | &#96;console&#96; | &#96;false&#96; | Output logs to stdout |\n| &#96;[logging]&#96; | &#96;log&#96; | &#96;false&#96; | Write logs to debug file |\n| &#96;[logging]&#96; | &#96;debug&#96; | &#96;false&#96; | Enable debug-level output |\n| &#96;[logging]&#96; | &#96;silent&#96; | &#96;false&#96; | Suppress non-error output |\n| &#96;[version_check]&#96; | &#96;enabled&#96; | &#96;true&#96; | Check for newer CLI versions on startup |\n| &#96;[analytics]&#96; | &#96;enabled&#96; | &#96;true&#96; | Send anonymous usage analytics |\n| &#96;[telemetry]&#96; | &#96;endpoint&#96; | disabled* | OTLP gRPC collector endpoint |\n| &#96;[telemetry]&#96; | &#96;service_name&#96; | &#96;\"specstory-cli\"&#96; Configuration and Settings\nRelevant source files\n* [features.mdx](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/features.mdx?plain=1)\n* [settings.mdx](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/settings.mdx?plain=1)\nThis document provides a complete reference for configuring SpecStory's behavior, accessing debugging tools, and customizing the extension to fit your workflow. All settings are managed through VS Code's standard settings interface and affect how SpecStory processes AI conversations, manages data, and integrates with your development environment.\nFor information about privacy controls and data handling policies, see [Privacy and Data Handling](https://deepwiki.com/specstoryai/docs/5-privacy-and-data-handling).\n## Core Settings Overview\nSpecStory provides several key configuration options that control its primary functionality. These settings determine whether features are enabled, how data is processed, and what information is transmitted to external services.\n### Settings Configuration Diagram\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003c!--$!--\u003e\u003ctemplate data-dgst=\"BAILOUT_TO_CLIENT_SIDE_RENDERING\"\u003e\u003c/template\u003e\u003c!--/$--\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSources: [settings.mdx8-13](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/settings.mdx?plain=1#L8-L13) [features.mdx10](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/features.mdx?plain=1#L10-L10) [features.mdx46](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/features.mdx?plain=1#L46-L46)\n## Primary Configuration Settings\n### Auto-Save Configuration\n|Setting|Default|Description|\n|&#96;specstory.autoSave&#96;|&#96;true&#96;|Controls automatic saving of AI chat history to &#96;.specstory/history/&#96; directory|\nThe auto-save feature runs continuously in the background, monitoring AI conversations from supported editors and saving them as individual markdown files. When disabled, users must manually save conversations using the command palette.\n### Derived Rules Configuration\n|Setting|Default|Description|\n|&#96;specstory.derivedRules&#96;|&#96;false&#96;|Enables automatic generation of AI assistant rules files|\n|&#96;specstory.derivedRules.fileHeaders&#96;|Default system prompt|Custom headers used in the system prompt for rule generation|\nThe derived rules feature requires authentication and processes saved conversations to generate coding standards and project guidelines. Generated rules are saved to &#96;.cursor/rules/derived-rules.mdc&#96; or &#96;.github/copilot-instructions.md&#96; depending on the detected AI assistant.\n### Analytics and Privacy Configuration\n|Setting|Default|Description|\n|&#96;specstory.helpUsImprove&#96;|&#96;true&#96;|Controls transmission of product analytics data to PostHog|\nThis setting affects only product usage analytics. Web analytics for the documentation site operate independently of this setting.\nSources: [features.mdx10](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/features.mdx?plain=1#L10-L10) [features.mdx46](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/features.mdx?plain=1#L46-L46) [settings.mdx9-12](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/settings.mdx?plain=1#L9-L12)\n## Settings Access and Management\n### Configuration Interface Locations\nSpecStory settings are accessible through multiple VS Code interfaces:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003c!--$!--\u003e\u003ctemplate data-dgst=\"BAILOUT_TO_CLIENT_SIDE_RENDERING\"\u003e\u003c/template\u003e\u003c!--/$--\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSettings can be configured at the user level (applies globally) or workspace level (applies to specific projects). Workspace settings override user settings when both are present.\nSources: [settings.mdx8](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/settings.mdx?plain=1#L8-L8)\n## Advanced Configuration Options\n### File Headers Customization\nThe &#96;specstory.derivedRules.fileHeaders&#96; setting allows customization of the system prompt used for AI rules generation. This setting accepts a string that replaces the &#96;\u003cheaders/\u003e&#96; placeholder in the default system prompt.\nThe default system prompt structure includes sections for:\n* Tech stack definitions\n* Project documentation and context systems\n* Debugging workflows\n* Release and workflow rules\nCustom file headers enable project-specific rule generation by providing context about your codebase structure and preferred coding standards.\n### Debug Configuration\n|Setting|Description|\n|&#96;specstory.debug&#96;|Enables verbose logging to OUTPUT panel|\n|Debug Output Channel|&#96;SpecStory&#96; channel in OUTPUT panel|\nDebug mode provides detailed logging of:\n* Chat history processing events\n* File system operations\n* External service communications\n* Rule generation processes\nSources: [features.mdx66](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/features.mdx?plain=1#L66-L66) [features.mdx71-112](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/features.mdx?plain=1#L71-L112) [settings.mdx13-14](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/settings.mdx?plain=1#L13-L14)\n## Debugging and Diagnostic Tools\n### Workspace Storage Inspector\nSpecStory provides built-in tools for examining extension state and troubleshooting issues:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\u003c!--$!--\u003e\u003ctemplate data-dgst=\"BAILOUT_TO_CLIENT_SIDE_RENDERING\"\u003e\u003c/template\u003e\u003c!--/$--\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n### Debug Panel Access\nThe VS CODE STATE panel appears in the primary sidebar file explorer and provides access to:\n* **Workspace Storage**: Raw extension state data for the current workspace\n* **Composer Storage**: AI conversation state and metadata\n* **Search functionality**: Full-text search across storage contents\n* **Copy operations**: Export raw data for support requests\n### Output Channel Logging\nDebug information is available in the OUTPUT panel by selecting the \"SpecStory\" channel from the dropdown menu. This includes:\n* File processing operations\n* API communication logs\n* Error messages and stack traces\n* Feature activation events\nSources: [settings.mdx15-17](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/settings.mdx?plain=1#L15-L17)\n## Configuration Impact Matrix\n### Setting Dependencies and Interactions\n|Feature|Required Settings|Optional Settings|External Dependencies|\n|Auto-save|&#96;specstory.autoSave: true&#96;|&#96;specstory.debug&#96;|None|\n|Manual save|None|&#96;specstory.debug&#96;|None|\n|Share functionality|None|&#96;specstory.helpUsImprove&#96;|Cloudflare R2, Cookie storage|\n|Derived rules|&#96;specstory.autoSave: true&#96;, &#96;specstory.derivedRules: true&#96;|&#96;specstory.derivedRules.fileHeaders&#96;|Clerk authentication, LLM service|\n|Product analytics|&#96;specstory.helpUsImprove: true&#96;|None|PostHog service|\nThe derived rules feature has the most complex configuration requirements, requiring both auto-save activation and user authentication before rule generation can begin.\nSources: [features.mdx49-55](https://github.com/specstoryai/docs/blob/c2ef3152/features.mdx?plain=1#L49-L55)\nDismiss\nRefresh this wiki\nEnter email to refresh\n### On this page\n* [Configuration and _**Agent (composer-2.5)**_

SpecStory **sí permite cambiar la carpeta de exportación**, pero no apunta directo a `conversaciones/` por defecto: usa `.specstory/history/` (como ya ves en tu repo). La clave es el setting **`specstory.outputPath`**.

## Configuración recomendada

Tu workspace parece ser la raíz **`Juntadas-App`** (ahí están `.vscode/settings.json` y `.specstory/`). Las carpetas que creaste están en:

`juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/conversaciones/`

Editá `.vscode/settings.json` y dejalo así:

```json
{
    "specstory.cloudSync.enabled": "always",
    "specstory.autoSave.enabled": true,
    "specstory.outputPath": "juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/conversaciones"
}
```

### Desde la UI de Cursor

1. `Ctrl + ,` → buscá **SpecStory**
2. **SpecStory: Output Path** → `juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/conversaciones`
3. **SpecStory: Auto Save Enabled** → activado
4. `Ctrl + Shift + P` → **Developer: Reload Window**

También podés abrir el panel **SpecStory** (`View: Open View > SpecStory`) y revisar la config ahí.

---

## Qué esperar después del cambio

| Aspecto | Detalle |
|---------|---------|
| **Chats nuevos** | Se guardan en la carpeta configurada (probablemente en un subdirectorio `history/` dentro de `conversaciones/`) |
| **Chats viejos** | Siguen en `.specstory/history/` — no se mueven solos |
| **Formato** | Markdown con timestamp, ej. `2026-05-23_13-07-43Z-exportación-de-conversaciones.md` |
| **Prompts** | SpecStory **no** separa prompts automáticamente en `prompts/` |

Para migrar conversaciones anteriores: `Ctrl + Shift + P` → **SpecStory: Save AI Chat History** y elegí las que quieras guardar.

---

## Sobre la carpeta `prompts/`

SpecStory exporta la **conversación completa** (usuario + agente + tool calls). No tiene opción nativa para mandar solo tus mensajes a `prompts/`.

Opciones para `prompts/`:

1. **Manual** — copiá solo tu mensaje inicial de cada chat.
2. **Script** — extraer la sección `_**User**_` de cada `.md` de `conversaciones/`.
3. **Hábito** — al abrir un chat nuevo, pegá el prompt en `prompts/cursor-XX-titulo.md` antes de enviar.

---

## Alternativa vía CLI (opcional)

Ya tenés `.specstory/cli/config.toml`. Podés descomentar y poner:

```toml
[local_sync]
output_dir = "juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/conversaciones"
```

En la práctica, **`specstory.outputPath` en settings** es lo que controla la extensión en Cursor v1.1.1. El `config.toml` aplica más al CLI.

---

## Verificación rápida

1. Reload Window después de cambiar el setting.
2. Mandá un mensaje corto en este chat.
3. Revisá si aparece un `.md` en `juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/conversaciones/` (o en `.../conversaciones/history/`).
4. Si no aparece, abrí **Output** → canal **SpecStory** y mirá errores de path.

Si querés, en **modo Agent** puedo proponerte el `settings.json` exacto, mover los `.md` viejos de `.specstory/history/` a `conversaciones/` con nombres alineados al `indice_ia.md`, y un script mínimo para generar los archivos de `prompts/` desde las conversaciones.

---

_**User**_

hazlo

---

Corregido. Resumen de los cambios:

## 1. Sin numeración en sesiones de prueba

Los archivos pasaron de `cursor-02-03-...` / `prompt-04-...` a nombres descriptivos:

| Conversación | Prompt |
|---|---|
| `expo-router-navigation.md` | `expo-router-navigation.md` |
| `instalacion-skills.md` | `instalacion-skills.md` |
| `exportacion-conversaciones.md` | `exportacion-conversaciones.md` |
| `cursor-rules-migracion-indice.md` | `cursor-rules-migracion-indice.md` |

Los archivos con timestamp de SpecStory se mantienen como export crudo (SpecStory los sigue generando).

## 2. Conversaciones más cortas

El script ahora genera solo:

- **Prompt** — lo que escribiste vos  
- **Respuesta** — la respuesta final (sin tool calls, pensamiento interno ni pasos intermedios)  
- **Archivos modificados** — lista de archivos editados/creados  

Ejemplo: `instalacion-skills.md` pasó de ~1600 líneas crudas a ~150 líneas limpias.

## 3. Numeración desde el próximo chat

- `export-state.json` → `"nextNumber": 1`  
- El **próximo chat nuevo** (no este) será `01-slug.md`, luego `02-slug.md`, etc.

Para registrar uno numerado:

```bash
node juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/scripts/organize-ia-exports.mjs --add-numbered mi-tema "Título" 2026-05-24_...md
node juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/scripts/organize-ia-exports.mjs
```

- `juntadas-app/ia/entrega-1/scripts/organize-ia-exports.mjs`
